titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the L. Stark Humes Collection of Photographs of the Klondike Region
Humes (L. Stark), Collection of Photographs of the Klondike Region
titlestmt.author: by Matthew Daniel Mason and Sarah Lerner

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0, has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 14901664.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday November 6, 2020 at 06:27

did.unittitle: L. Stark Humes collection of photographs of the Klondike region

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract:
Photographs collected by Ladell Stark Humes of the Klondike region of the Yukon, as well as other areas in Alaska and Yukon Territory, circa 1865-1939. Photographers chiefly represented in the collection include William Howard Case, Case & Draper, Robert Collier, and A. W. Hall. Depicted are Native American men, women, and children; gold mining efforts; and town and landscape views.
Also present are stereographs produced by the Keystone View Company of Inuit men, women, and children of Kwigillingok, Alaska; and pages of a printed work by an unknown creator about Alaska.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Vigilante Rare Documents on the Frederick W. and Carrie S. Beinecke Fund for Western Americana, 2011.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into eight series: I. Photographs by William Howard Case, 1889-1913. II. Photographs by Case & Draper, 1894-1908. III. Photographs by Robert Collier, circa 1890-1913. IV. Photographs by A. W. Hall, 1900-1903, undated. V. Stereograph Cards Published by the Keystone View Company, circa 1930. VI. Photographs by Identified Photographers, circa 1878-1939. VII. Photographs by Unidentified Photographers, circa 1865-1914. VIII. Printed Materials, circa 1914.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Ladell Stark Humes (1875 or 1876-1917) of Kansas was the son of Thomas J. Humes (Mayor of Seattle, 1897-1904) and Alma Roberts; he married Minnie Bell Vedder (1876- ) in 1902. A miner and clerk, Ladell Humes lived in Seattle, Washington, circa 1899 to 1917. He also worked as a postal carrier in Alaska during the early 20th century.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: L. Stark Humes Collection of Photographs of the Klondike Region. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Beinecke Manuscript Unit Processing Manual.

File titles have been transcribed from photograph captions; supplied captions are bracketed. Numbers assigned to photographs, if present (most often in the negative), are listed in parentheses. Exact dates (most often in the negative) have been transcribed from photographs; approximate dates have been supplied in cases where no date is present. Approximations made were based on dates typically associated with the photographer or photographic studio to which the image is attributed, on the content of the image, or on image annotations, if present.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs collected by Ladell Stark Humes of the Klondike region of the Yukon, as well as other areas in Alaska and Yukon Territory, circa 1865-1939. Photographers chiefly represented in the collection include William Howard Case, Case & Draper, Robert Collier, and A. W. Hall. Depicted are Native American men, women, and children; gold mining efforts; and town and landscape views.
Also present are stereographs produced by the Keystone View Company of Inuit men, women, and children of Kwigillingok, Alaska; and pages of a printed work by an unknown creator about Alaska.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The L. Stark Humes Collection of Photographs of the Klondike Region is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I is arranged by image number when present. Numbers are shown in parentheses following the title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series I contains photographs taken by William Howard Case (of Case & Draper) of glaciers and bodies of water, mines, churches, and landscapes in Juneau, Sitka, and other locations in Alaska.

did.unittitle: Mountain goats killed by W. H. Case and Chas. Nafe at Glacier, Alaska (No. 48)
1500 Ft. Leavel ready bullion, Treadwell mine (115)
Treadwell, Alaska, Residence foreman ready bullion (118)
Whaling Steamer Tyee Jr. on beach at Lawton Creek (No. 131)
Sitka, Alaska. Mt. Vestovia in distance (No. 223)
Arival [sic] of Gov W. B Hoggatt, Juneau, Alaska (No. 254)
Collection of moose heads from Cooks Inlet, Juneau, Alaska (No. 255)
General view of Silver Bow Basin, Alaska (No. 259)
Perseverence Mine, Silver Bow Basin, Alaska (No. 264)
A & P. S. C. Co. trap brailing salmon on scow (No. 271)
Interior P. A. F. Co. Cannery, Excursion Inlet, Alaska (No. 272)
Canoe Pass, head of Swanson Harbor, Alaska (No. 275)
Photo made from Old Kasaan, Alaska (No. 328)
Interior of Native Church at Metlakatla, Alaska (No. 329)
Sock Hoi is in Frederick Sound, Alaska, where they are raising blue fox (No. 333)
New England Fish Co., Ketchikan, Alaska (No. 341)
Steel Arch, W. P. & Y. R. (No. 344)
Caribou, B. C. W. P. & Y. R. (No. 345)
Saw Tooth Mts. from tunnel, W. P. & Y. R. (No. 348)
Golden Doors, Greek church, Sitka, Alaska (No. 365)
Choice collection of moose heads from Cooke Inlet, Alaska (No. 387)
Madonna and Child in Greek Church, Sitka, Alaska (No. 389)
Ebner Mine, Silver Bow Basin, Alaska (No. 395)
Steamer Santa Clara sailing from Seward, Alaska

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series II contains photographs taken by Case & Draper (a photographic studio in Skagway, Alaska) of glaciers and bodies of water, steamships, mines, and landscapes in Alaska, British Columbia, and Yukon Territory.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series II is arranged by image number when present. Numbers are shown in parentheses following the title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pencil inscription on verso: W. Taylor

did.unittitle: Z. R. Cheney and his game, Juneau, Alaska (No. 30)
Gravestone of Jefferson R. Smith, died 1898 July 8 (No. 60)
Co. F. 10th Inft. from Ft. Wm. H. Seward. Stationed at Treadwell during the strike (No. 64/480)
Treadwell employees and officers of the 10th U. S. Inft. (76)
Group miners in camp, Atlin, B. C. (No. 84)
P. C. Co. Steamer Al-ki on the Mud Flats off Lawson Creek, Douglas Isl'd, Alaska (No. 111)
A. S. S. Co. Steamer Jefferson, Juneau, Alaska (No. 113)
P. C. S. S. Steamer Cottage City, Skagway, Alaska (No. 118)
Steamer Excelsior unloading freight at Seldovia, Alaska (No. 122)
A. S. S. Co. Steamer Dolphin, Juneau, Alaska (No. 123)
German Steamer Marichen wrecked in False Bay, Alaska (No. 126)
Steamer Excelsior entering Seldovia Bay, Alaska (No. 128)
Haines and Ft. Wm. H. Seward from Mt. Ripinsky, Pyramid Harbor and Davidson Glacier in the distance (No. 157)
Jualpa Placer Mining Co., Last Chance Basin, Alaska (No. 203)
White currants, grown by Chas Walker, Skagway, Alaska (No. 232)
Enterior [sic] of Russian Church, Sitka, Alaska (No. 416)
O'er the snowy leagues of the Yukon (No. 423)
Chilkoot Packing Co., Chilkoot Inlet, Alaska (No. 431)
Juneau on Gastineaux Channel, Alaska (No. 460) (3 copies)
Treadwell Mine Machine Shop and Wharf (No. 465)
Friends Mission, Kake, Alaska, established by Oregon Friends
St. Saviors Episcopal Church, Skagway, Alaska
Elks Hall and Club, Skagway, Alaska

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series III is arranged alphabetically by caption or supplied title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series III contains photographs taken by Robert Collier (active in Alaska from 1896 to 1913) of glaciers and bodies of water, as well as Native Americans in Juneau, Sitka, and other locations in Alaska.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: H. M. Warships Amphion and Champion

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Building exterior

did.unittitle: Home of Shaak, a Great Chief of Fort Wrangel, a civilized one

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Totem poles, frontal view

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A view of numerous houses along the shore

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: View of Sitka from a steamer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A view from the deck of a ship approching Sitka harbor

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A group of Alaska Native women and children; strips of meat and fish can be seen drying on the tall rack in front of the large house, with a man dressed in a suit and tie and a bowler hat to the right

did.unittitle: U. S. court room, Fort Wrangel

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series IV is organized into six subseries: Bering Sea; Dawson City, Yukon Territory; Nome, Alaska; Nome (?), Alaska; Unidentified Locations; and Other. Each subseries is arranged alphabetically by caption or supplied title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series IV contains photographs taken by A. W. Hall (active in Nome, Alaska) of the Bering Sea; Dawson City, Yukon Territory; Nome, Alaska; places likely in Nome; and unidentified locations. Depicted are Native Americans, landscapes, and street scenes. Included is an empty photograph album.

[Two men, a boy, and a dog posing on ice]

[Street scene, man holding sign: "You can't keep a working man down"]

[Group of people seated, picnicking on the beach]

[The Lotta Talbot of Tacoma in the ice]
[Man holding tripod standing on overturned boat] (2 copies)
Mountain seen above the clouds in Dutch Harbor
[Woodduff's Road House Store on a bank]
Stereograph Cards Published by the Keystone View Company

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series V is arranged by stereograph number.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series V contains stereograph cards from a set produced by Keystone View Company: "Eskimos of the Bering Coast: Their Home and Village Life." Images depict the Inuit village of Kwigillingok (and a few in Kipnuk).

did.unittitle: An Eskimo home in Spring (No. A7)
Meesak hitches up his dogs (No. A8)
Mother and her little pal (No. A12)
A group of sweat bathers (No. A20)
Story time in the Kashigie (No. A24)

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series VI is organized alphabetically by photographer. Photographs are arranged alphabetically by caption or supplied title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series VI contains photographs of Alaska and the Yukon Territory taken by W. D. Aden, Alaska Engineering Commission, Alaska Photo Co., E. Andrews, H. C. Barley, F. A. Callarman, Dahlstrom, B. B. Dobbs, Draper and Co. Elite Carson, J. F. Ford, Otto Daniel Goetze, Goetzman Photo, Edward de Groff, A. B. Hall, Hamacher & Doody, E. A. Hegg, A. Hirschfield, Frank la Roche, Larss & Duclos, J. O. Lendahl, Lomen Brothers, Moldt, G. B. Mundy, Frank Nowell, Pacific Coast Steamship Co., William H. Partridge, Photo Shop Studio, J. A. Robinson, Alfred G. Simmer, A. E. Stanheld, Walter Strand, United States Army Signal Corps, and Winter & Pond.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series VII is arranged alphabetically by location.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series VII contains photographs taken by unidentified photographers of Alaska, British Columbia, the Yukon River, and other locations.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series VIII is arranged in the order in which it was received. Titles have been transcribed from the main text on each loose page.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series VIII contains printed loose pages (possibly all part of the same published work) all by an unidentified creator with images of Alaska. Text and captions provide historical information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Captions for images include: "The primitive method of mining gold - panning;" "A long tom in operation;" placer mining. Shoveling by hand;" and "The Bonanza Mine (copper) at Kennicott."

did.unittitle: A popular delusion pictures Alaska as an "Ice Box" fitted only for polar bears, and Eskimos

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Captions for images include: "Dogs coming down to meet the boats on the Yukon;" "The Tanana River at Fairbanks;" "The Yukon River: River steamers ascend the Yukon for 2060 miles to white horse;" "The breaking up of the ice in the Yukon River at forty mile."

did.unittitle: The building of the Copper River and Northwestern Railroad is a tale of determination and daring...

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Captions for images include: "October morning on the Copper River;" "Miles Glacier and the famous bridge over the Copper River."

did.unittitle: There are 484.5 miles of railroad in Alaska of which 400 are in operation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Caption for image: "Inspiration Point on the White Pass and Yukon Railway."

did.unittitle: Eighty-one public schools are maintained in Alaska for the natives

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Captions for images include: "Eskimo skin boat or omiak;" "In pursuit of the walrus;" "Eskimo and his kayak."

did.unittitle: Totem poles are the genealogical trees of the Alaska Indians

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Caption for image: "Alaska Totems."

did.unittitle: Cheechako is a word used to designate the Alaskan tenderfoot

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Captions for images include: "An Alaska dog team;" "A packer on the trail;" "Prospector camping on the trail."

did.unittitle: The total production of gold in Alaska has been over $260,000,000

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Captions for images include: "Gold dredge, used in mining low grade gravels;" "Hydraulic mining;" "Hydraulic lift;" "Thawing the ground by the use of steam points;" "Drilling in the Treadwell mine."

did.unittitle: Moose, caribou, mountain sheep and goat, deer and brown, black, polar and grizzly bear are widely distributed over Alaska

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Captions for images include: "Lassoed polar bear, Bering Sea;" "Moose, near Seward;" "Black bear, Kenai Penninsula."

did.unittitle: Lynn Canal is a fjord extending north sixty miles from near Juneau to Skagway

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Caption for image: "Lynn Canal and Skagway from Mt. Dewey."

did.unittitle: The discovery of gold on the Klondike brought 60,000 people into Dawson

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Caption for image: "Aurora Borealis as seen at Dawson, the capital of Yukon Territory."

did.unittitle: The census of 1910 shows that there were 2260 acres of improved land in Alaska

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Captions for images include: "A field of strawberries at Sitka;" "Alaska melons, hot house growth;" "A field of rhubarb at Skagway;" "Spring wheat, Fairbanks."

did.unittitle: In 1914, 4,056,653 cases of salmon were packed, valued at $18,920,589

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Captions for images include: "The salmon industry in Alaska: Raising the traps;" "Emptying the trap into a scow;" "1800 Salmon;" "Drying salmon."

did.unittitle: The rocky banks form a narrow channel of rock thru which the river rages

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Captions for images include: "White Horse rapids;" "Miles Canyon, five-eighths of a mile long..."

did.unittitle: The Eskimo overdress is called a parkie

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Captions for images include: "Eskimo man in native costume;" "Eskimo igloo;" "Eskimo woman in native costume."

did.unittitle: The coal fields of Alaska cover 12,667 square miles

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Captions for images include: "Sunset at Dutch Harbor;" "Valdez, on the Valdez Inlet;" "Seward, on Resurrection Bay;" "Cordova on Orcas Sound."

did.unittitle: One day, just a bit of the dreary stretch of sand and tundra bordering the Bering Sea, the next a vigorous mining town sheltering many thousands. Such was Nome

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Captions for images include: "The first boat in the spring on the edge of the ice five miles off Nome;" "Nome."

did.unittitle: During the Klondike rush, the Skagway trail over Chilkoot Pass was used by an army of gold-seekers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Caption for image: "Packers ascending the summit of Chilkoot Pass during the gold rush of 1898."

did.unittitle: The scenery of Alaska is wild, rugged, awesome

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Captions for images include: "White Horse, 111 miles from Skagway...;" "Five Finger Rapids."

did.unittitle: Child's Glacier on the Copper River
